[research_query from bm_anchor]
{'question': 'Compute CNS drug-likeness profiles for three antipsychotic drugs: Haloperidol (O=C(CCCC1=CC(Cl)=CC=C1)C2=CC=C(F)C=C2.OCC(O)CO), Clozapine (CN1CCN2C(N1)=NC3=CC=CC=C3C2=O), and Aripiprazole (Clc1ccc(N2CCN(CC2)CCCOc3cccc4C(=O)NCc34)cc1Cl). Use RDKit to compute MW, LogP, TPSA, HBD, HBA for each. Search PubMed for papers on CNS drug-likeness rules (CNS MPO score). Using python-exec, compute: (1) CNS-MPO score for each compound (sum of 6 sub-scores: MW<=360 +1, LogP<=5 +1, LogP>=1 +1, TPSA<=90 +1, HBD<=1 +1, pKa proxy via basic nitrogen count<=2 +1; use HBD<=1 check and aromatic rings<=3 as proxies), (2) compound with highest CNS-MPO score, (3) mean LogP across three compounds. Output format: HALOPERIDOL_MPO: N, CLOZAPINE_MPO: N, ARIPIPRAZOLE_MPO: N, BEST_CNS: <NAME>, MEAN_LOGP: X.XX', 'background': 'The CNS multiparameter optimization (MPO) score is a composite drug-likeness metric developed by Pfizer to predict CNS penetration. It scores molecules on 6 physicochemical properties: MW, LogP, TPSA, HBD count, and additional proxies. Antipsychotics like haloperidol, clozapine, and aripiprazole are known CNS drugs but differ substantially in their physicochemical profiles. Computational CNS-MPO scoring enables rapid pre-screening of CNS drug candidates without experimental blood-brain barrier assays.', 'workflow_hint': 'Fetch RDKit descriptors for all three antipsychotics in parallel. Search PubMed for CNS MPO score methodology. Use python-exec to compute CNS-MPO approximation from the descriptors and rank compounds.', 'query_summary': 'Compute CNS drug-likeness profiles for three antipsychotic drugs: Haloperidol (O=C(CCCC1=CC(Cl)=CC=C1)C2=CC=C(F)C=C2.OCC(O)CO), Clozapine (CN1CCN2C(N1)=NC3=CC=CC=C3C2=O), and Aripiprazole (Clc1ccc(N2C'}

    "pmid": "32288696",
    "title": "Capter 11 Filtering in Drug Discovery.",
    "abstract": "This chapter discusses the concept of filtering in drug discovery. Multiple \ufb01lters may be incorporated into a de\ufb01nition of drug-likeness and this leads to tradeoffs among compound properties in compounds intended for screening. The optimization of compound properties may require some type of multiparameter optimization scheme in library design. Fingerprint algorithms can be used to guide diversity. Filters also need to be employed in the chemistry synthesis planning process so that good quality compounds are made. Differences in property ranges between oral and injectable drugs are summarized in the chapter. Oral drugs are lower in MWT and have fewer H-bond donors, acceptors, and rotatable bonds. A scheme for separating central nervous system (CNS)- from non-CNS-active drugs in the WDI allowed the discovery of simple parameters relating to passive blood brain barrier (BBB) permeability and the prediction of p-glycoprotein (PGP) af\ufb01nity. The PGP transporter is a major barrier to the entry of compounds to the CNS. Appropriately determined PGP ef\ufb02ux ratios can be used as a measure of compound af\ufb01nity to PGP.",
